title: Persona switch mirrors a legacy trust-by-email session shortcut
impact_summary: Session trust shortcuts can accumulate through email suffixes, role strings, or client-side assumptions that were meant to be temporary.
why_this_matters: The boundary between a harmless demo shortcut and a real authz regression is something teams frequently underestimate.
evidence_fixture: trust-boundary panel plus disabled legacy admin session helper
suggested_patch_summary: Keep client personas untrusted and move any privileged decision to server-side session verification.
verification_summary: Verified as partial because the repo intentionally has no real auth provider or persistent session store.
replay_regression_note: Confirm persona switching remains local-only and the legacy session shortcut stays disabled.
